name: Job Application CRM v5.3 - JobStreet Submission Fix

filters.q: in:inbox -from:me -subject:"New Job Application Added" -subject:"Job Application Status Updated" -subject:"📌 New Job Application Logged" -subject:"🔄 Job Application Status Updated"

parameters.text:
=Classify and extract one job-application lifecycle email for Ronald Amancio.

QUALIFY only messages about a job Ronald already applied to:
- application received/submitted/under review
- recruiter follow-up
- assessment or coding challenge
- interview invitation or schedule
- shortlisting
- request for information
- rejection or withdrawal
- offer, hiring, background check, or onboarding

DO NOT QUALIFY job alerts, recommended jobs, advertisements, newsletters,
promotions, invitations to apply, privacy/security/account messages, or unrelated mail.

Extract only facts explicitly present. Never guess.

Use exactly one applicationStatus:
Applied, Application Received, Under Review, Recruiter Contacted,
Assessment Requested, Interview Requested, Interview Scheduled,
Shortlisted, Additional Information Requested, Rejected, Withdrawn,
Offer Received, Hired, Unknown.

Status mapping:
- submitted/acknowledged → Application Received
- assessment/test → Assessment Requested
- interview invitation without date → Interview Requested
- interview with confirmed date/time → Interview Scheduled
- declined/not progressing → Rejected
- formal offer → Offer Received
- onboarding/hiring confirmed → Hired
- non-application → Unknown

For non-application messages, return empty strings for all extracted text fields.
Identify the actual employer, not the job board or ATS.
Use received date as dateApplied for a clear application confirmation when no date is stated.
Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD.
Return only the connected structured output.

Sender: {{ $json.senderName }} <{{ $json.senderEmail }}>
Subject: {{ $json.subject }}
Received: {{ $json.receivedDate }}

Body:
{{ $json.cleanedEmailBody }}

parameters.inputSchema: {"type": "object", "properties": {"isJobApplicationEmail": {"type": "boolean"}, "confidence": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 1}, "jobBoard": {"type": "string"}, "companyName": {"type": "string"}, "jobTitle": {"type": "string"}, "jobDescription": {"type": "string"}, "salaryOffered": {"type": "string"}, "jobDatePosted": {"type": "string"}, "dateApplied": {"type": "string"}, "applicationStatus": {"type": "string", "enum": ["Applied", "Application Received", "Under Review", "Recruiter Contacted", "Assessment Requested", "Interview Requested", "Interview Scheduled", "Shortlisted", "Additional Information Requested", "Rejected", "Withdrawn", "Offer Received", "Hired", "Unknown"]}, "recruiterName": {"type": "string"}, "recruiterEmail": {"type": "string"}, "applicationUrl": {"type": "string"}, "reason": {"type": "string"}}, "required": ["isJobApplicationEmail", "confidence", "jobBoard", "companyName", "jobTitle", "jobDescription", "salaryOffered", "jobDatePosted", "dateApplied", "applicationStatus", "recruiterName", "recruiterEmail", "applicationUrl", "reason"], "additionalProperties": false}

parameters.content:
## Job Application CRM v5 — Hybrid Parser

### Why Version 5 uses fewer Groq tokens

Predictable application confirmations are parsed with JavaScript first.

Examples commonly handled without AI:
- Indeed application submitted
- LinkedIn application sent
- JobStreet application confirmation
- Greenhouse/Lever/Workday acknowledgement
- Thank you for applying
- We received your application

Groq is reserved for:
- interview invitations and schedules
- assessments and coding challenges
- recruiter follow-ups
- shortlisting
- rejection messages
- offers and onboarding
- ambiguous or unusual application updates

### Required Google Sheet headers

Application Key
Job Board
Company Name
Job Title
Job Description
Salary Offered
Job Date Posted
Date Applied
Last Activity Date
Application Status
Recruiter Name
Recruiter Email
Application URL
Email Subject
Received Date
Last Updated
Message ID
Confidence Score

### Live workflow

Publish only after testing.

New emails:
1. pass through the newsletter/job-alert filter;
2. attempt deterministic parsing;
3. use Groq only when deterministic extraction is unsafe;
4. update Google Sheets;
5. sort newest-first;
6. send notifications.

### 90-day backfill

Run **Run 90-Day Backfill** manually.

The backfill:
- searches likely application emails from the last 90 days;
- processes oldest-first;
- uses deterministic parsing whenever possible;
- waits 8 seconds between historical emails;
- suppresses notifications;
- prevents older messages from replacing newer statuses;
- sorts Google Sheets once after completion.

### Important limitation

A digest containing several applications still produces at most one application row.
Individual application confirmation and update emails are handled more reliably.


### Version 5.1 correction

Build Application Record was repaired to remove a malformed duplicated JavaScript expression.

Application Key is now empty unless both company name and job title are available.
Qualified Application checks that Application Key is not empty.


### Version 5.2 model change

Groq Chat Model now uses:
llama-3.1-8b-instant

Reason:
- predictable confirmations are already handled without AI;
- the smaller model is sufficient for remaining classification/extraction;
- it has a larger free-tier daily token allowance than llama-3.3-70b-versatile;
- output is limited to 500 tokens;
- email content sent to AI is limited to 2,200 characters;
- temporary rate-limit failures retry up to four times with a 60-second delay.

Use llama-3.3-70b-versatile only after the historical backfill is complete if you need higher accuracy for future ambiguous live emails.


### Version 5.3 correction
Recognizes JobStreet subjects such as:
- Your application was successfully submitted
- Application was successfully submitted
- Successfully submitted

Also parses:
"Your application for [JOB TITLE] was successfully submitted to [COMPANY]"

These confirmations bypass Groq.

filters.q: newer_than:90d in:anywhere -from:me -category:promotions -category:social -subject:"New Job Application Added" -subject:"Job Application Status Updated" -subject:"📌 New Job Application Logged" -subject:"🔄 Job Application Status Updated" {"application submitted" "application received" "application confirmation" "thank you for applying" "thanks for applying" "your application was sent" "application update" "regarding your application" "interview invitation" "interview scheduled" "technical assessment" "coding challenge" "selected to proceed" "move forward with the hiring process" "we regret to inform you" "not be progressing with your application" "offer letter" "pleased to offer you"}
